new_value: {"description":"DESIGN CHOICE (not a bug): Polynomial partition function path (saha_boltzmann.py:179-185) uses pre-tabulated coefficients that already include natural IP truncation. When IPD lowers the continuum, the polynomial path does not adjust. This is a known approximation — polynomial coefficients cannot easily incorporate plasma-density-dependent IPD. The level-summation fallback path (lines 205-214) correctly applies IPD truncation. Consider: add a correction factor U_corrected = U_poly * (1 - correction(delta_chi)) or force level summation when IPD delta_chi \u003e threshold."}
old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-improved-6g8","title":"BUG: Partition function polynomial path ignores IPD lowered continuum","description":"In saha_boltzmann.py:179-185, when polynomial partition function coefficients exist, calculate_partition_function() returns immediately without applying max_energy_ev truncation. Only the level-summation path (lines 205-214) honors the lowered ionization potential. This means IPD affects the Saha exponential but NOT U(T) when polynomial coefficients are available, creating an inconsistency that biases ionization ratios. Fix: either apply an IPD correction factor to the polynomial PF, or always use level summation when IPD is active.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-21T17:35:01Z","created_by":"Brian Squires","updated_at":"2026-03-21T17:35:01Z"}

new_value: {"description":"GPU-accelerate Voigt profile computation in cflibs/radiation/profiles.py (833 LOC). Current: Humlicek W4 approximation computed per-line in a Python loop (lines ~200-350). Target: Replace the per-line loop with JAX vmap over all spectral lines, enabling GPU batch evaluation. Implementation: 1. Add jax import guard (like existing pattern in cflibs/manifold/): try/except ImportError with HAS_JAX flag 2. Create jax_voigt_batch(wavelengths, centers, sigma_g, sigma_l) using jax.scipy.special.erfc or custom W4 3. Wrap with @jax.jit for compilation caching 4. GEMINI DEEP RESEARCH RECOMMENDATION: Discard RANSAC and sigma-clipping for the JAX path — their dynamic array shapes conflict with XLA compilation (no dynamic shapes allowed in jit). Instead, compose Optax huber_loss with JAXopt batched solvers. Implementation: 1. pip install optax jaxopt (add to pyproject.toml optional deps) 2. Replace per-element numpy.polyfit loop with batched approach:    - Stack all elements x_arrays (E_k values) and y_arrays (ln(I*lambda/gA)) into padded tensors    - Use optax.huber_loss as the robust loss function (replaces sigma-clip/RANSAC/Huber M-est)    - Minimize with jaxopt.LBFGS or jaxopt.GradientDescent (batched across elements via vmap) 3. Temperature extraction: T_m = -1/(kB * slope_m) unchanged 4. Fallback: existing NumPy loop when JAX/optax unavailable 5. Unit test: compare JAX batch fit vs sequential NumPy fit (rtol=1e-5) Key insight: single compiled GPU tensor operation replaces iterative sequential CPU loops. Expected speedup: \u003e1000x. BoltzmannPlotFitter API and BoltzmannFitResult dataclass unchanged.","notes":"Task complete: Updated .swarm-progress.txt with full analysis of boltzmann.py and boltzmann_jax.py, implementation plan for JAX-vectorized Huber fitting, and file modification notes. old_value: {"id":"CF-LIBS-improved-e9f","title":"Fix element identification false positives (precision=0.35)","description":"ALIAS identification produces too many false positives (precision 0.346). Atmospheric/contaminant elements (N, O, Na, K, Ca, Ar) are detected in nearly every spectrum. Solutions: (1) Implement atmospheric-element exclusion filter, (2) Raise ALIAS detection threshold, (3) Use multi-line consistency check — require ≥3 lines per element, (4) Implement physics-informed filtering via Boltzmann R² per element. Benchmark: LIBS-aware precision should reach \u003e0.7.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T04:46:25Z","created_by":"Brian Squires","updated_at":"2026-03-24T04:46:25Z"}

{"id":"CF-LIBS-improved-sbk","title":"Fix solver convergence failures (T clamped at 49922K)","description":"Multiple minerals show solver converging to T=49922K (max clamp), indicating the Boltzmann slope is positive or near-zero. This happens when: (1) Too few lines per element for reliable fit, (2) Self-absorbed lines flatten the Boltzmann plot, (3) Wrong lines identified. Solutions: (1) Require minimum 4 lines per element-stage for fit, (2) Apply self-absorption exclusion for resonance lines, (3) Better initial T guess from line ratio methods. Benchmark: \u003c10% of spectra should hit temperature clamp.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T04:46:25Z","created_by":"Brian Squires","updated_at":"2026-03-24T04:46:25Z"}

{"id":"CF-LIBS-improved-h8i","title":"Improve self-absorption handling for resonance lines","description":"Current CD-SB correction exists but ablation shows minimal impact (RMSEP 0.4501 vs 0.4541). Issues: (1) CD-SB may not be triggering for the right lines, (2) Resonance line exclusion may be more effective than correction, (3) Escape factor thresholds may need tuning. Solutions: (1) Auto-detect resonance lines (low E_k, high gA), (2) Exclude lines with τ\u003e0.5 from Boltzmann fit, (3) Use BRR-SAC (Li 2019) as alternative to CD-SB. Benchmark: ablation should show \u003e5% RMSEP improvement with SAC.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"squires.b@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T04:46:27Z","created_by":"Brian Squires","updated_at":"2026-03-24T04:46:27Z"}
